title: Customizable landing page for documentation home
description: Add an admin-editable landing page shown at the public site's root, separate from the sections/pages tree, so an instance can present a welcoming home screen instead of dropping visitors directly into a section.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated `instance_settings`-style table/row stores the landing page's content (reuse the `blocks`-based content model where practical, tied to a well-known singleton id rather than a `tab_id`)
Admin can edit the landing page's content using the same Tiptap editor (TASK-25) used for regular pages
Public site root route (`/`) renders the landing page content instead of redirecting straight into the section tree
If no landing content has been set yet, a sensible default (e.g. a simple heading plus a prompt for the admin to customize it) is shown instead of a blank page

steps.details: Add a `landing_page` concept: either a special well-known `pages`/`tabs` row (e.g. a reserved slug) or a dedicated single-row table storing its own `blocks`-equivalent content. Prefer reusing the existing `tabs`/`blocks` tables with a reserved sentinel `page_id`/`tab_id` to avoid duplicating the block-serialization logic from TASK-31.
Add `/admin/settings/landing-page` reusing `ContentEditor` (TASK-25) bound to the landing page's reserved tab id, including autosave (TASK-32) and an explicit publish action analogous to TASK-34.

steps.description: Render the landing page at the public root
steps.details: Update the public layout's `/` route (TASK-52) to fetch and render the landing page's latest published content via the same `PageRenderer` (TASK-50) used elsewhere, instead of any section/page.

steps.description: Add a sensible default for unset landing content
steps.details: If the landing page has never been published, render a minimal default (e.g. the instance's design system name and a link into the first section) rather than a blank screen.

Using Playwright: as admin, edit and publish landing page content; visit `/` unauthenticated and confirm it renders; as a fresh instance with no landing content published, confirm the default state renders instead of blank.

technicalNotes: Reusing the existing tabs/blocks/revisions machinery for the landing page (via a reserved sentinel id) avoids building a second parallel content-storage system just for one page — worth the minor conceptual awkwardness of a 'page-less tab'.
